titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of NRS GD55/134

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=403 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:02:30.839706+00 | translation moa=404 status=live modified=2021-04-06 15:02:32.291977+00

p.span:
Ansel’ de Wittune vniuersis sancte Matris ecclesie filiis tam presentibus quam futuris Salutem . Sciant tam presentes
quam posteris me dedisse et hac mea carta confirmasse deo et domui de Melros . et monachis ibidem deo seruientibus
in perpetuam elemosinam totam illam meam peteriam que est Molope et berope et herdstrete que diuidit terram
de Molle et de hunum pro anima dauid . Regis . et henrici comitis . et Malcolmi Regis . et pro salute Willelmi Regis et dauid
fratris eius . et pro anima mea et vxoris mee et liberorum meorum . Itaque monachi de Melros tenebunt predictam petariam in pu
ram et perpetuam elemosinam quietam et liberam ab omnibus auxiliis et omnibus consuetudinibus . Et ego et heredes
mei warantizabimus predictis monachis eandem peteriam erga omnes homines imperpetuum . Preterea dedi eis in perpetuam
elemosinam in Bosco meo de Molope buschiam quantum unus equus potest portare ad grangiam suam de hunedun
singulis annis a pascha usque ad Natiuitatem sancte Marie eadem libertate qua peteriam eis dedi .
Hiis Testibus . Simone archidiacono Glasguensi . Ric’ Decano de hastenesdene . Petro persona de Morebotle .
Willelmo persona de hunum . Iohanne filio orm . et Willelmo filio eius . Willelmo de Sumeruilla . Rogero de
Wiltuna . Willelmo de Lund’.

p.cl: Anselm of Whitton sends greeting to all sons of Holy Mother Church both present as well as to come.

Let present as well as future know me to have given and by this my charter to have made firm to God and to the monastic house of Melrose, and to the monks serving God in that place, in perpetual alms my whole peatery which is [between] Mowhope and Berhope and ‘Herdstrete’ which divides the land of Mow and Hownam, for the soul of King David, and Earl Henry, and King Malcolm, and for the salvation of King William, and David his brother, and for my own soul and my wife’s and my childrens’ souls. And so the monks of Melrose shall hold the said peatery in pure and everlasting alms, quit and free from all aids and all customs. And I and my heirs shall warrant to the said monks the same peatery in respect of all men for ever. In addition I have given to them in everlasting alms in my wood of Mowhope as much firewood as one horse can carry to their grange of Hownam each year from Easter to the Nativity of Saint Mary, by the same liberty by which I gave them the peatery.
